title: Derived graphics geometry (dist)
description: Schema for components/dist/graphics/graphics.json: the derived, vendored color-preserving artwork read-surface produced by scripts/graphics/derive-graphics-svg.js. A sibling of icons.json for the artwork tier (brand illustrations, the pyramid mark, partner logos): each entry carries geometry (viewBox, body) and, unlike icons, keeps multicolor fills/strokes/gradients verbatim instead of normalizing to currentColor. graphics.minProperties:1 guards against an empty set shipping after a mass export loss.

_schema_version.description: Substrate schema version. Pinned at 1; bump only on a schema-incompatible change to the file shape.

_meta.description: Provenance stamp on the CI-derived file. Documents the source script and generation metadata.

examples.do_not_edit: Edit components/src/graphics-svg.json; CI regenerates this file.

auto_generated.description: True when this file was produced by the derive script.

source.description: Relative path (from repo root) of the script that generated this file.

do_not_edit.description: Guidance string signalling that manual edits will be overwritten on next derive run.
examples: Edit components/src/graphics-svg.json; CI regenerates this file.

graphics.description: Map of artwork slug -> color-preserving SVG geometry. Each entry holds a viewBox and inner SVG body markup, arbitrary multicolor content (fills, strokes, gradients, internal fragment refs) preserved verbatim from the Figma export. minProperties:1 ensures the set is never empty.

actian-pyramid.body: <path fill="url(#gradient)" d="M20 4 36 34H4Z"/><defs><linearGradient id="gradient" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop stop-color="#0F5FDC"/><stop offset="1" stop-color="#1B7F3B"/></linearGradient></defs>

viewBox.description: SVG viewBox: four space-separated numbers (minX minY width height).

body.description: Inner SVG markup (paths/groups/gradients/defs), no root <svg>. Multicolor content is kept verbatim, not normalized to currentColor. Arbitrary SVG markup is allowed; an internal fragment reference (url(#id), href="#id") is legal, only an external resource reference is rejected by scripts/validate/validate-graphics.js at derive time.
